qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Uncontrolled resource consumption via unbounded configuration parsing
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding reports unbounded configuration parsing and recursive processing in loadFile/load/flatten/overlay, while the advisory root cause is a startsWith()-based forbidden-key bypass that enables constructor.prototype prototype pollution.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory concerns prototype pollution in convict <=6.2.4 caused by using String.prototype.startsWith() to screen forbidden keys before config.set('constructor.prototype.*'), which can be bypassed by overriding startsWith. The only final qualifying finding concerns denial of service from unbounded parsing and recursive merging of large or deeply nested configuration input.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-44fc-8fm5-q62h describes Object.prototype pollution through config.set('constructor.prototype.polluted', 'yes') after overriding String.prototype.startsWith.

uncontrolled_resource_consumption/vulnerability_report.json lists affected code locations packages/convict/src/main.js:428-436, 584-590, 76-119, and 354-370 for file loading, load(), flatten(), and overlay() resource consumption.
The finding does not describe the forbidden-key startsWith() guard, constructor.prototype pollution, or a bypass of prototype-pollution defenses.
blockers_to_higher_tier: No advisory-matched qualifying finding was present, so no advisory-specific PoC execution was permitted or needed.
